title: AdminApiErrorEnvelope
description: Standard error envelope for all 4xx/5xx responses from the agentic-sandbox Admin/Fleet API. Conforms to RFC 7807 Problem Details for HTTP APIs (application/problem+json) with three operator-focused extension fields: `code`, `instance`, and `trace_id`. This envelope is independent of any A2A error model — it applies only to Surface 1 (admin/fleet) operations defined in ADR-022.

type.description: URI reference identifying the problem type. Dereferencing it SHOULD provide human-readable documentation for the problem type. When the member is not present, its value is assumed to be 'about:blank'.

title.description: Short, human-readable summary of the problem type. Should not change between occurrences of the same type.

status.description: HTTP status code generated by the origin server for this occurrence of the problem.

detail.description: Human-readable explanation specific to this occurrence of the problem.
examples: No instance with id '8a3f...' exists in the fleet registry.

instance.description: URI reference that identifies the specific occurrence of the problem. In agentic-sandbox this is typically the request path including the offending instance id. (RFC 7807 standard field; not to be confused with sandbox 'instance' nouns.)

code.description: Stable machine-readable error code. Operator tooling (sandboxctl, dashboard) may key on this. Codes follow `domain.condition` form.

trace_id.description: Server-assigned correlation id (W3C Trace Context trace-id, 32 hex chars) for cross-referencing logs and traces. Always present when telemetry is enabled.

errors.description: Optional list of field-level validation errors. Present on 422 / validation.failed responses.

examples: must be one of: qemu, docker

examples.title: Instance not found
examples.detail: No instance with id '8a3f8b94-1c4e-4d2b-9c1a-1ad44e6b0dfe' is registered.
